text: Cross-lingual task generalization: An exploratory study Fine-tuning cross-lingual language models has not been explored deeply in previous works. This paper takes the first step in this direction by introducing BLOOMZ and mT0 — a family of very large language models that can scale up to follow human provided instructions in a variety of languages. In order to fine-tune such models, we present xP3 — an extensive prompt collection of 46 languages and 16 diverse NLP tasks. We show that fine-tuning large-scale multilingual models over xP3 leads to strong cross-lingual task generalization abilities, and that the resulting models can follow human provided instructions in unseen target languages for an individual task much better than the previous state-of-the-art model mTk-Instruct. We also look into model scaling and the effects of combining tasks, and we find that multilingual multitasking leads to strong and highly competitive zero-shot generalization. Our work sets a new direction towards global AI instruction sharing.
